item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image could not be sourced, with the closest source, here, showing no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a tough one. I love the concept of a violin that steals the voice of its victims and leaves them with only musical notation. I like the fact that the victims can still communicate through the instrument even more. Sadly the article has a number of issues with its composition (aptly enough) as mentioned above that drag it down. Going to give a +1 in the hope of a rewrite of the piece that maintains the core concept.

item.title: Pretty Good
item.content:encoded:
Admittedly, bumping this article up to “Really Good” status wasn’t based just on the article itself, also on the implications I pieced together after thinking on it. Those implications being this could’ve been a serial killer’s tool (like SCP-668), one where the killer used the tool (either by shaking it or talking) to silence his targets before falling upon them. The theme of 13 and the fact that 1301-2 is made of black horse hair seem to be likely ties to the Bible, but I don’t quite get how the horse hair being the horse hair of Famine would make sense (though it could be used to illustrate that 13 is in the case the angel number and that 1301 was a gift from God).
In terms of the article itself, it has a Series-1 vibe from its length, description is good, the test logs are a great addition, and its effects are interesting and unsettling. Overall a good one.

+1

item.title: Wording Issues
item.content:encoded: I know this post is 7 years old, but a lot of wording is off and it has tone issues. Why don’t they use D-class for this? Did they not exist in 2012?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The way this is written is very awkward which makes for a very strange read.

Redundant phrases like

inside a secured, soundproofed box inside of a secured, soundproofed chamber

and tone issues like

Any personnel or individuals

are what initially set me off from this.

To add on to this, the main anomaly is not interesting. It reminds me of that sloth skip that I can't remember the # of which makes you lose motor functions but that manages to tell a horrifying story with the interview log. This, however, feels very lackluster in story. Not to mention that there's already a lot of slips that make you lose motor functions so this just gets lost in the sea of them.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral vote, not terrible but not particularly interesting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is not very well written, but I think there's a good article hiding underneath it. Up-voted with the hope that it'll see a rewrite.

For the record, I think the SCP-012 cross reference in the experiment log was well-picked. There's not that many musical SCPs and this one can render notation into a voice through playing so I don't feel this was at all forced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's ok so I upvoted it but this

Prior authorization is required to handle either SCP

sounds off. I don't think that researchers call scips that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was ready to neutral vote, the downvoted after reading the logs. The cross reference was unnecessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is nice, but the execution does too little with it, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has anyone heard from the original author? I'd do an edit for tone but I'm not sure if I should.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems fairly original, but overall it is a bit 'meh'. Also the experiment log seems to contain moderate levels of 'meh' as well. I'm staying neutral for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "of a crimson hue"? Awful flowery for an SCP document. Also, can we get the test logs in an expandable section?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well-written, but I just cannot bring myself to like this concept. I've seen this done so many times and this doesn't add anything new.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The musical aphasia is good, the violin playing human voices is okay, but not together. Maybe try splitting this up into two SCPs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm, I like the idea— but the image seems a bit generic, and some of the writing is in need of stylistic editing, which bugs me. Neutral for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who's to say they did?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. The experiment log takes it from mediocre to pretty good. My main complaint at this point is: how did they get the violin solo from SCP-012 without driving somebody insane?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the new experiment log, and the change so writing becomes musical notation that only this violin can "read". Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. The last entry of the experiment log especially gave me the good heebie jeebies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure! Such vocal ranges have actually been accomplished in real life, albeit uncommonly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So if you play a D or G two bars above the staff.. It sounds like a man still?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here, I tried to spice it up a little. Did it work?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ….. Out of everything, I'm curious as to how, exactly, they determined the violin's strings were made of spider silk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've actually had that as a thought for a potential experiment; another one was using it to play SCP-012 and some strange effect resulting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe playing a full song with it might cause the voices to pronounce some interesting sentence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll agree that I'm into the violin playing human voices, screaming, crying, etc., but the replacing of people's voices with violin music doesn't really do anything for me. There's a good idea here, but it's not yet reached that state. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of a violin that plays human voices and effected subjects speak in violin voices, however I think your execution needs some improving (meaning you have to spice it up a little bit). No votes for now.

By the way, in my opinion, how the Foundation should do with those effected subjects should be mentioned in the Special Containment Procedures section instead of the SCP Description section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe the Devil's fiddle isn't made of gold…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It could be presented a bit better, but I like the general idea.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit boring, not very well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually rather like this. It reads a little awkward, but not enough to prevent an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. I was tempted to upvote due to my numerous years of playing violin, but it felt off. The violin playing human voices was enough to not downvote after the "YOU TALK LIKE VIOLIN!!1!1one"

So yeah, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Done. Is there a particular reason you're downvoting it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please add a rating module so I can downvote this.
